item.title: Boardroom ready: Building a business case for tech investment
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover how we empower association and nonprofit professionals with best practices, frameworks, and real-world examples to help you build leadership buy-in for future-ready tech decisions.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Boardroom Ready: Building a Business Case for Tech Investment

a:2:{s:6:"item-0";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"1690";s:14:"presenter_name";s:14:"Tirrah Switzer";s:21:"presenter_description";s:55:"Vice President of Product Marketing, Momentive Software";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-1";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"1689";s:14:"presenter_name";s:15:"Rebecca Achurch";s:21:"presenter_description";s:48:"Chief Executive Officer, Achurch Consulting, LLC";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}}

Gaining board approval for new software can feel like an uphill battle in today's economic climate.

With 56% of association professionals focused on boosting staff productivity and 51% prioritizing tech upgrades and integrations in the year ahead, it's clear that investing in the right tools is key to long-term success.

Getting new technology approved is a powerful opportunity to strengthen your organization’s foundation for the future. Watch now to discover how we empower association and nonprofit professionals with best practices, frameworks, and real-world examples to help you build leadership buy-in, ensure long-term stability, and make confident, future-ready tech decisions.

item.title: 2025 learning insights to power your growth
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Compare your strategies and programs to evolving member needs — and uncover new ways to grow loyalty, engagement, and revenue.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
Are your learning programs keeping pace with what members expect today? According to the latest Association Trends Study, 50% of members now prioritize ongoing training, a clear signal that career development is top of mind. The study reveals key insights into how associations can better support member growth, leverage technology, and deliver greater value through education.

Compare your strategies and programs to evolving member needs — and uncover new ways to grow loyalty, engagement, and revenue. Join Jesse Shore, Learning Programs Expert at Momentive Software, as she shares the top takeaways from the latest Association Trends Study.

item.title: The true cost of data for associations
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Find out how to better understand the hidden costs of poor data—and how to turn your data strategy into a true asset for your association.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Watch The True Cost of Data for Associations webinar. Learn to turn your data strategy into a true asset.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"1937";s:14:"presenter_name";s:9:"Evan Reid";s:21:"presenter_description";s:85:"Senior Director of Analytics, The American Speech-Language-Hearing Association (ASHA)";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-1";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"1936";s:14:"presenter_name";s:12:"Wes Trochlil";s:21:"presenter_description";s:36:"Owner, Effective Database Management";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-2";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"1935";s:14:"presenter_name";s:10:"Rob Miller";s:21:"presenter_description";s:47:"SVP of Association Strategy, Momentive Software";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}}

For today’s associations, data is more than an operational asset—it’s a strategic imperative.

From data security vulnerabilities to financial losses caused by outdated information, poor data management has real and far-reaching consequences. Association leaders are increasingly recognizing that outdated data strategies can no longer keep pace with modern demands.

Watch our webinar, The True Cost of Data for Associations, to better understand the hidden costs of poor data—and how to turn your data strategy into a true asset for your association.
This session will provide valuable insights into:

a:4:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:11:"custom_icon";s:12:"box-bx-chart";s:18:"custom_description";s:87:"Building a culture of data hygiene through regular audits and governance best practices";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:11:"custom_icon";s:15:"box-bx-bullseye";s:18:"custom_description";s:73:"Addressing the rising risks of data breaches, fraud, and compliance gaps ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:11:"custom_icon";s:13:"box-bx-trophy";s:18:"custom_description";s:114:"Quantifying the financial impact of inaccurate data—missed renewals, declined transactions, and lost engagement ";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:11:"custom_icon";s:11:"box-bx-file";s:18:"custom_description";s:102:"Leveraging clean, reliable data to drive personalized member experiences and strategic decision-making";}}

item.title: Transform your association’s learning programs into a revenue-generating hub
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Join our webinar to learn how Path LMS can help you create non-dues revenue streams with seamless eCommerce tools, powerful analytics, and strategic content development services.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
Associations are facing shrinking budgets and increasing pressure to diversify revenue streams. 73% of organizations say increasing non-dues revenue is critical. Start creating an additional revenue stream from educational content, breaking dependence on membership fees and supporting sustainable, long-term growth.  Path LMS provides the technology and tools to turn your education programs into sustainable revenue drivers. 

Join Path LMS by Momentive Software for a 15 minute webinar to see how you can easily deliver high-quality, on-demand learning while generating non-dues revenue.

item.title: Aptify in Action: Discover flexible member management for large associations
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Join this live webinar to see Aptify in action and learn how large associations use flexible member management to scale operations and boost engagement.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Aptify in Action: Flexible Member Management | Live Webinar
Join this live webinar to see Aptify in action and learn how large associations use flexible member management to scale operations and boost engagement.

Large associations and unions manage unique complexity—and your technology should help simplify it, not slow you down. Aptify is built specifically to support large, complex associations or unions, offering the flexibility, scalability, and power needed to streamline operations across your entire organization. 

In just 30 minutes, you’ll get a clear look at how Aptify brings efficiency and visibility to membership management, events, finance, and more—so your team can stay focused on delivering value to your members.

a:4:{s:6:"item-0";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:104:"Discover Aptify’s end-to-end capabilities from membership to ecommerce, and everything in between
";}s:6:"item-1";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:75:"The flexible platform that adapts to complex organizational structures
";}s:6:"item-2";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:62:"Real examples of processes that become faster and simpler
";}s:6:"item-3";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:76:"Features that help your association scale without adding operational burden ";}}

This demo is ideal for association leaders, membership, IT, and operations teams, who are evaluating next‑generation association management technology.

Reserve your seat today and discover how Aptify can transform the way your association manages members, data, and day‑to‑day operations.

item.title: Smarter Association Management with Nimble AMS
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Join this live webinar to learn how Nimble AMS helps associations manage members smarter, streamline operations, and improve engagement.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Smarter Association Management with Nimble AMS | Live Webinar
Join this live webinar to learn how Nimble AMS helps associations manage members smarter, streamline operations, and improve engagement.

See how modern associations streamline, automate, and grow 

Join us for a concise, high‑impact product overview of Nimble AMS—built on Salesforce and designed to help associations deliver exceptional member experiences while working smarter.

a:4:{s:6:"item-0";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:97:"Seamless member management: Manage dues, events, and engagement in one connected platform ";}s:6:"item-3";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:104:"Process automation & no‑code tools: Cut manual work and adapt quickly—no developers required ";}s:6:"item-2";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:109:"Data‑driven insights: Use real‑time analytics to guide strategy and uncover revenue opportunities ";}s:6:"item-1";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:110:"Community engagement: Empower members to connect and collaborate through a built‑in online community ";}}

In this short demo, you’ll see how Nimble AMS helps you deliver a seamless member experience—through automatic upgrades, no-code automation, and flexible configuration that keeps your system modern without added complexity. We’ll also showcase powerful features that support membership management, process automation, data analytics, and a built-in online community platform to help your organization connect and engage with members.

Don’t miss this chance to see how Nimble AMS helps associations streamline operations and drive mission‑forward growth.

item.title: Resilient Revenue: Smart Ways Nonprofits Can Thrive in Any Economy
item.dc:creator: colleen.bernum@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Explore how nonprofit organizations are diversifying income streams and strengthening donor relationships in today's fluctuating economy.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: GiveSmart Live Webinar | Nonprofit Revenue Streams
Explore how nonprofit organizations are diversifying income streams and strengthening donor relationships in today's fluctuating economy.

Economic uncertainty doesn’t have to mean uncertainty for your mission.

Join us for an hour-long, idea-packed webinar designed to help nonprofit professionals rethink how they generate revenue and connect with supporters. We’ll explore how organizations like yours are diversifying income streams, strengthening donor relationships, and building long-term sustainability in today's fluctuating economy.

a:5:{s:6:"item-0";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:77:"How to identify and expand new revenue streams beyond traditional fundraising";}s:6:"item-1";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:60:"Creative ways to engage more donors without increasing spend";}s:6:"item-2";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:68:"Practical strategies to reduce reliance on any single funding source";}s:6:"item-3";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:74:"Real-world examples of nonprofits successfully adapting in uncertain times";}s:6:"item-4";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:74:"Simple frameworks you can use to evaluate and prioritize new opportunities";}}

All attendees will gain—and leave with a copy of—insights inspired by Momentive's nonprofit revenue workbook, providing revenue diversification and implementation strategies long after the webinar ends.
Register now to join us live on June 24! Can't attend? No worries! Register anyway, and a copy of the recording will be delivered to your inbox.

Summer is a season of change — and so is the NetForum Summer '26 (Release 40) release. This update brings meaningful improvements across your platform, from a major transition in how payments are processed to modernized member-facing forms and the debut of Momentive IQ — the intelligence platform that will power every future innovation in NetForum. Join our product team for a live walkthrough of what's new, what it means for your staff and members, and how to make the most of everything included in this release. 

The NetForum Summer '26 Release Overview Webinar takes place on July 16 at 11:30 a.m. ET and is designed to give your team a clear, confident path into these updates — covering what's changing, what's available now, and what's coming next.
Don't miss your opportunity to see these updates in action and understand what's available now and what's coming next.
Register now to get ready for the NetForum AMS Summer 26 (Release 40).

item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover how to build stable, ROI-driven events in uncertain times using agile design, smart tech, and strategic engagement to boost registration and retention.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
In times of uncertainty, events have the power to ground and inspire. In this free webinar, discover how thoughtful investments in event technology can provide more than just efficiency—they offer a foundation for connection, stability, and long-term impact.

Whether you're navigating tighter budgets, evolving priorities, or shifting stakeholder expectations, this session will equip you with practical tools and fresh perspectives to not just weather change—but lead through it. Together, we’ll explore how technology, community, and strategic collaboration can help you create experiences that matter now more than ever.

item.title: Technology Performance in Nonprofits and Associations: How Data-Driven Adoption Drives Growth and Future-Readiness
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover how technology decisions are impacting organizational performance, growth, and future-readiness, and how you can make sure your organization doesn't fall behind.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: a:2:{s:6:"item-0";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"1746";s:14:"presenter_name";s:12:"Shannon Reed";s:21:"presenter_description";s:58:"Senior Director of Engagement, The Electrochemical Society";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-1";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"1690";s:14:"presenter_name";s:14:"Tirrah Switzer";s:21:"presenter_description";s:43:"VP of Product Marketing, Momentive Software";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}}

While others are still talking about digital transformation, we now have the data to show what actually works. Join us for a research-based look at how technology decisions are creating measurable differences in organizational performance—and why some nonprofits and associations are thriving while others fall behind.

We'll share concrete findings from our previously published nonprofit research and preview results from our upcoming associations study that reveal the performance gaps between what member organizations think they're delivering and what their members truly experience. This webinar isn’t just about adopting technology—it's about optimizing it for measurable impact.
Watch now and get the complete nonprofit report and early findings from our associations research.
Fill out the form to save your seat

<script charset="utf-8" type="text/javascript" src="//js.hsforms.net/forms/embed/v2.js"></script>

    formId: "200f1a80-0895-4232-a3f4-731cb998a077",
    region: "na1",
    sfdcCampaignId: "701Ph00000ki1ynIAA"
  });
</script>

a:6:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:13:"insight_title";s:20:"The performance data";s:19:"insight_description";s:171:"Nearly half of nonprofits grew revenue last year, but organizations with strategic technology use averaged 5.14 revenue streams while others struggled to diversify funding";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:13:"insight_title";s:40:"Technology readiness predicts confidence";s:19:"insight_description";s:159:"Despite funding fears, many nonprofit professionals feel positive about their future, and their technology preparedness is the strongest factor in that outlook";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:13:"insight_title";s:25:"AI implementation reality";s:19:"insight_description";s:121:"Only 21% of nonprofits use AI, but those who do report measurable value in content creation, marketing, and data analysis";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:13:"insight_title";s:18:"The perception gap";s:19:"insight_description";s:145:"First look at association survey data showing how staff assumptions about technology performance don't match what members are really experiencing";}s:6:"item-4";a:2:{s:13:"insight_title";s:35:"What high performers do differently";s:19:"insight_description";s:144:"Practical approaches to member experience, AI policies, and the technology decisions that separate successful organizations from struggling ones";}s:6:"item-5";a:2:{s:13:"insight_title";s:20:"The next three years";s:19:"insight_description";s:138:"Why some organizations won't survive without strategic technology optimization—and what successful implementation looks like in practice";}}
Discover how technology choices affect growth and performance. Learn how to make sure your organization stays future-ready.
Discover how tech decisions impact growth and performance

item.title: 2024 Association Trends Study reveal webinar
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: The results are in! Check out our deep dive into the comprehensive findings of the 2024 Association Trends Study.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
A new era of associations

As technology continues to rapidly evolve, adapting and adjusting to the changes along with the new member experience is crucial. Learn how association professionals are leveraging technology to provide greater member value, addressing the constantly changing needs of members, and how their attitudes and outlooks about technology have changed.

Momentive Software (formerly Community Brands) and Edge Research share our findings of the 2024 Association Trends Study. We’ll examine the transformational power of technology and its role in shaping the future of member engagement and association growth.
This webinar will cover the study results, collected from thousands of members and hundreds of association leaders. What you can expect to learn:

item.title: What Associations Need to Know About Tax Changes

item.title: Advancing Professional Excellence: Best Practices for Continuing Professional Education for CPAs
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Join us on-demand to learn best practices to elevate your continuing professional education (CPE) program with engaging, high-quality content in a variety of delivery modes to meet your learners’ needs.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Best Practices for Continuing Professional Education | Momentive
Learn best practices to elevate your CPE program with engaging content across multiple delivery modes.

On average, a certified public accountant (CPA) must complete approximately forty hours of continuing professional education (CPE) each year. Your learning management system (LMS) needs to provide an adequate amount of engaging, high-quality content in numerous delivery modes in an intuitive platform that meets their needs. We’ll share proven best practices and effective strategies to enhance your CPE program.

a:4:{s:6:"item-0";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:134:"Providing multimodal content delivery through both live and on-demand content to cater to varied learner preferences and career stages";}s:6:"item-1";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:161:"Optimizing your content catalog by balancing just-in-time and just-in-case learning options to address immediate needs and future requirements of professionals ";}s:6:"item-2";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:160:"Streamlining attendance tracking and credit delivery to easily manage multiple completion requirements and credit submission across different learning mediums ";}s:6:"item-3";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:153:"Maximizing content utility to reach broader audiences through rebroadcasts and repurposing in-person programming for webcasts and virtual presentations ";}}
Don’t miss this opportunity to elevate your continuing education program and ensure your organization remains a leader in professional development.

Jessica Metzler
Director of Operations
Freestone LMS

item.title: NextGen Learning: Strategies for Attracting & Engaging Gen Z Learners
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Meet Gen Z learners’ demand for continuing education and career advancement opportunities with our strategies to capture, engage, and retain these young professionals.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: NextGen Learning: Strategies for Attracting & Engaging Gen Z Learners
Capture the next generation of learners. Discover effective strategies to attract and engage Gen Z learners during this 15-minute on-demand webinar.

Association research shows that 57% of Gen Z-ers engage with their professional organizations at least once a week. They are more tech savvy than previous generations, are hungry for information, and are accustomed to having instant access to data. These early career members are more apt to seek out training and certifications that will aid in career advancement.
In this session, we will discuss proven strategies to capture, engage, and retain the next generation of learners, including:

Enhance CLE programs with the right LMS. Learn practical tips and best practices to streamline content delivery.

item.title: Leave a Legacy Like Springsteen: How Digital Accessibility & Gifting Can Secure Your Association’s Future
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn how associations can boost member retention and financial stability through digital accessibility and legacy gifting strategies, with insights on engaging Baby Boomers and implementing accessible learning systems. Discover practical approaches to secure your organization's future while creating lasting value for members.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Boost member retention and financial stability with digital accessibility, legacy gifting, and accessible learning systems.

In this enlightening webinar, we explore how associations can leverage digital accessibility and legacy gifting to enhance member retention, increase lifetime value, and secure a thriving future. Drawing inspiration from Bruce Springsteen’s perspective on leaving a lasting impact, we’ll delve into strategies that cater to the aging Baby Boomer demographic while creating new revenue streams for your association.

Join us to discover how an accessible Learning Management System (LMS) can transform your organization’s approach to member engagement and financial sustainability. We’ll discuss practical ways to implement digital accessibility, attract legacy gifts, and create a win-win situation for both your association and its long-standing members.
Don’t miss this opportunity to future-proof your association and create a legacy that resonates for generations to come!

item.title: Finding Your Best-Fit AMS: How Associations Are Investing for the Future
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn how to enhance member engagement, streamline operations, and grow your association by identifying your best-fit AMS capabilities.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
Selecting the right AMS is one of the most important technology decisions your association will make—especially as investment in management systems continues to rise. According to the 2025 Association Trends Report, 32% of professionals plan to invest more in their management systems this year (up from 22% in 2024), the highest percentage in recent years. And with 62% of association professionals citing upgrading or integrating new technology as a top priority, now is the time to carefully weigh the pros and cons of available AMS options to find the best fit for your organization. 

In this on-demand webinar, you’ll gain practical guidance on how to identify the AMS capabilities that can streamline operations, enhance member engagement, and position your association for future growth. You’ll walk away with a clear framework to confidently choose a system that supports both your current needs and long-term strategy.

item.title: Next-Gen Career Centers: Research Insights on AI, Member Expectations, and Revenue
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Get research-backed insights into how to leverage AI and emerging technologies in your career center to drive revenue and long-term growth.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
The career center has become mission-critical—far more than just a job board. It’s where associations win or lose on member engagement and revenue growth. But time is running out. Rapid advances in technology, widening generational gaps in usage, and growing debates around AI are raising member expectations faster than most associations can keep pace. The question is no longer if you adapt, but how quickly you can act to deliver career value that secures your future.
In this research-backed session, we’ll share the latest insights from the 2025 trends study, exploring how AI and emerging technologies are reshaping career centers. Discover how leading associations are leveraging their career platforms to meet rising member demands, unlock new revenue opportunities, and secure long-term growth.

item.title: Strategic Budgeting for Impact in 2026: Aligning Mission, Money, and Modernization
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Watch this on-demand webinar to learn how successful nonprofits and associations kept their budget agile during 2025 and are better prepared for the uncertainty of 2026.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: a:2:{s:6:"item-0";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"1935";s:14:"presenter_name";s:20:"Rob Miller, MPA, CAE";s:21:"presenter_description";s:47:"SVP, Association Strategy at Momentive Software";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-1";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"4248";s:14:"presenter_name";s:20:"Tom Harlow, CPA, CAE";s:21:"presenter_description";s:76:"Interim Chief Finance Officer at ASAE: The Center for Association Leadership";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}}

Are your priorities in place to meet your organization's needs and those of the people you serve? Learn how successful nonprofits and associations kept their budget agile during 2025 and are better prepared for the uncertainty of 2026. 

Our latest research shows that member and donor retention are top priorities for both association and nonprofit professionals over the next year. But in order to retain these sources of revenue, mission-driven organizations must reevaluate the budgetary strategies that shaped their 2025 plans. It’s time to move from the traditional budget models that once shaped the association and nonprofit industries and start to consider models that can optimize budget flexibility and innovation.
This webinar will provide you with:

a:5:{s:6:"item-0";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:125:"Insights from our annual 2025 Nonprofit and Associations Reports with real budget data and benchmarking trends year-over-year";}s:6:"item-1";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:59:"How to shift from reactive to proactive budgeting practices";}s:6:"item-2";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:124:"Tips to create adaptive, scenario-based budgets to withstand volatility (membership fluctuations, funding uncertainty, etc.)";}s:6:"item-3";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:91:"Best practices incorporating tech investments into the budget as a hedge against disruption";}s:6:"item-4";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:157:"Strategies for allocating a dedicated portion of annual budgets into funding reserves specifically for innovation, experimentation, and modernization efforts";}}

Whether you’re the CFO of an enterprise association, a nonprofit marketing professional, or an executive director of a small-staff organization, our session is one you don’t want to miss. In a fluctuating economic environment, preparing your budget to be agile and successful is more important than ever.

Ace Your Next Nonprofit Audit | Momentive Software

Audits may not be a nonprofit’s favorite thing, but they are a common and sometimes necessary way to examine financial statements, analyze business operations, and review internal processes. How can you ensure your nonprofit will be successful the next time you’re facing an audit?

We’ve partnered with a panel of accounting experts, including Michael Gellman, CPA, CGMA, Paul Preziotti, CPA, Tyler Mosley, CPA, CFE, and Stan Duncan, CPA, to provide everything you need to know to successfully complete your next audit.

item.title: Nonprofit Accounting: Collaboration, Synergy & Buy-In
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Join this webinar for a practical, actionable discussion about collaboration, synergy and workflows in nonprofit accounting.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Join this webinar for a practical, actionable discussion about collaboration, synergy and workflows in nonprofit accounting.

Nonprofit accounting teams can face various challenges that make collaboration complicated. How can you go beyond the status quo of ‘what we did last year’ and optimize processes and transparency with key stakeholders?

Some factors that can foment siloes or suboptimal accountability include lack of clear vision or buy-in; varying skill sets or training; constraints with technology, time, or staffing; strategic misalignment; or minimal visibility into adjacent departments.
Join this webinar for a practical, actionable discussion about collaboration, synergy and workflows in nonprofit accounting. We will cover:

item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Join experts A. Michael Gellman, CPA, CGMA and Paul Preziotti, CPA for a discussion on nonprofit reporting frameworks and best practices.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Join experts A. Michael Gellman, CPA, CGMA and Paul Preziotti, CPA for a discussion on nonprofit reporting frameworks and best practices.

Financial reporting can be taken for granted. Sometimes, a nonprofit’s finances are deemed overly complicated, and reports don’t get the attention they deserve. But since timely, accurate, complete, and actionable reports are essential for year-end accounting, budgeting, board meetings, and strategic planning, how can you ensure your reporting effectively communicates your financial position?

Join experts A. Michael Gellman, CPA, CGMA and Paul Preziotti, CPA for a helpful discussion on nonprofit reporting frameworks and best practices.

item.title: Gaining Board Approval for New Accounting Software
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: As you’re seeking board approval for new initiatives, it’s essential to see the proposal through your key stakeholders’ lens. Your board is working to ensure every decision made is financially sound, mission-aligned, and supported by evidence.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Board proposals must be mission-aligned, financially sound, and supported by evidence to gain approval.

Lacking visibility into your finances? Relying on workarounds each month to manage data and accounting? Need to be more confident about compliance? If your organization is working through any of these challenges, it may be time to have a conversation about getting better accounting tools.

Securing support for accounting technology investment demands a clear, strategic, and well-supported business case that resonates with stakeholders at every level, especially your board. A compelling business case aligns the problem, solution, and outcomes with the mission and long-term sustainability of the organization.

Our 2025 Nonprofit Trends Report highlights that while 96% of nonprofits use technology for accounting, many organizations still struggle with optimizing their use to maximize mission-driven impact.

Watch now to learn how to build a board-ready business case for nonprofit tech investments that drive operational efficiency, data integrity, and long-term sustainability.
We’ll break down what matters most to financial stakeholders and board members, including:

item.title: Reduce Errors in Your Accounting and Reporting
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Join us for a helpful discussion of tried-and-true and forward-thinking methods for reducing errors in your accounting and reporting.

Reducing errors and ensuring accurate financial reporting are critically important to save time, manage resources, understand the financial position, and remain compliant (especially for federal grants) and transparent. Despite the importance, research shows that more than half of accounting professionals make multiple errors every month, and nearly 1 in 5 accountants make financial errors every day.¹

Join us for a helpful discussion of tried-and-true and forward-thinking methods for reducing errors in your accounting and reporting.

Can you believe the year is almost over? Time flies. Are you prepared to close the books?

End-of-year accounting activities will be here soon. Attend this webinar to discuss best practices for annual reporting tasks, tax preparation, budgeting, and learn how to set 2023 strategic priorities effectively. Getting year-end right helps with audit prep, composing your annual report, communicating key metrics on mission delivery, and so much more.

item.title: Head Start on 2025 Accounting & Fundraising Trends
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn about the latest accounting and fundraising trends for nonprofits in this informative webinar. Discover ideas to grow revenue, reduce errors, and maintain compliance.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn nonprofit accounting and fundraising trends in this webinar to grow revenue, reduce errors, and maintain compliance.

73% of nonprofits need more revenue today than they did two years ago¹. Finance leaders agree that an innovative approach to fundraising, events, and donor stewardship is necessary to generate more funding.

The sector is trending towards more collaboration between accounting and development teams, and boards are increasingly looking for deeper financial insights to support strategic decisions.

How can your organization stay ahead of the curve? Join us for a discussion of finance and fundraising trends to watch this year.

item.title: Simplify Audit Prep with MIP Accounting
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Nonprofit audits don’t have to be stressful. Discover how MIP Fund Accounting simplifies compliance, reduces risk, and helps your team prepare audit-ready reports in minutes.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Nonprofit audits made simple. MIP Fund Accounting ensures compliance, reduces risk, and prepares audit-ready reports efficiently.

With funding tied to tighter regulations and higher expectations, finance leaders must prioritize accuracy, efficiency, and real-time reporting to remain proactive in meeting shifting expectations. According to our study, 33% of nonprofit organizations have lost funds due to compliance issues.

Beyond securing future funding opportunities, remaining in compliance makes audits smoother, strengthens your nonprofit’s reputation, and ensures your organization can sustainably demonstrate good fiscal stewardship.
Watch now and learn how MIP helps you:

a:4:{s:6:"item-0";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:62:"Track every dollar across multiple funds and funding sources ";}s:6:"item-1";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:59:"Generate clean, audit-ready reports in minutes—not days ";}s:6:"item-2";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:60:"Maintain built-in internal controls and approval workflows ";}s:6:"item-3";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:59:"Automate documentation and ensure compliance at every stage";}}

Designed to track unlimited funds and manage your financial books with ease, MIP is the nation’s leading cloud-based, SaaS fund accounting solution.
If you’re ready to reduce audit stress, improve accuracy, and free your team from manual processes, this session will show you how MIP can support you every step of the way.

item.title: Nonprofit Audits: Internal Controls, Prep, Budgets and More
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Join us for a strategic and pragmatic discussion covering year-round audit-readiness and preparation, including best practices and more.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Join us for a strategic and pragmatic discussion covering year-round audit-readiness and preparation, including best practices and more.

Nonprofit accounting teams spend about 20-30 hours per year on audit preparation – and between time and resource constraints, numerous funding sources and related transactions, restricted funds, regulatory compliance, board oversight and more – there are lots of moving parts.

How can your team implement a proactive approach to audits that makes the process efficient, seamless and easily replicable?
Join us for a strategic and pragmatic discussion covering year-round audit-readiness and preparation, including:

a:5:{s:6:"item-0";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:65:"Best practices for each stage: pre-audit, fieldwork and wrap-up ";}s:6:"item-1";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:84:"Tips for developing tailored internal controls and conducting periodic evaluations ";}s:6:"item-2";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:78:"Building effective frameworks for documentation, fund tracking and reporting ";}s:6:"item-3";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:77:"Better collaboration with internal stakeholders, the board and your auditor ";}s:6:"item-4";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:10:"And more! ";}}

How Top-Performing Nonprofits Grow Revenue Through Optimism, Flexibility, and Futureproofing

Join us for an exclusive first look into the latest research study. We’ve gone deep into the data and surfaced the insights you need to navigate today’s nonprofit landscape—and tomorrow’s. This isn’t just a study, it’s a story of how today’s top-performing nonprofits are able to grow revenue through optimism, flexibility, and futureproofing.

Whether you’re leading fundraising, managing operations, or driving mission outcomes, you’ll leave with fresh ideas, new benchmarks, and the inspiration to level up your impact.

item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Join us for a discussion of 5 specific ways to save time when accounting and reporting across multiple funding sources, programs and services.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: To manage an organization’s finances, most accounting professionals spend at least 30% of their time each week manually handling data and wrangling different tools. Not surprisingly, over 80% say becoming more efficient is the key to overcoming challenges presented by persistent staffing shortages.*

So, join us for a discussion of 5 specific ways to save time when accounting and reporting across multiple funding sources like grants, fundraising, donations or programs and services so that you have more time for strategic planning and analysis:

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:0:"";s:14:"presenter_name";s:14:"Cara Dickerson";s:21:"presenter_description";s:53:"VP of Client Success, Momentive Software (Moderator) ";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-1";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:0:"";s:14:"presenter_name";s:10:"Art Taylor";s:21:"presenter_description";s:46:" CEO, Association of Fundraising Professionals";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-2";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:0:"";s:14:"presenter_name";s:14:"Renèe Croteau";s:21:"presenter_description";s:62:"Senior Director, Distinguished Events, American Cancer Society";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}}

Is your organization looking for ways to deepen connection and strengthen community during the busiest fundraising season of the year?

This on-demand webinar brings together three partner organizations—the Association of Fundraising Professionals (AFP), the American Cancer Society (ACS) and Momentive Software—for an authentic conversation about building belonging across mission-driven communities.
In this collaborative discussion, you'll discover:

a:5:{s:6:"item-0";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:96:"How to foster community among fundraising professionals facing high-pressure year-end campaigns ";}s:6:"item-1";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:113:"Strategies for keeping teams, volunteers, and donors connected during Giving Tuesday and peak fundraising season ";}s:6:"item-2";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:81:"Ways technology and data can strengthen belonging without losing the human touch ";}s:6:"item-3";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:92:"How partnerships between associations, nonprofits, and service providers can amplify impact ";}s:6:"item-4";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:10:"And more! ";}}

Learn how partnerships across mission-driven organizations can create stronger connections, expand reach, and lighten the load during your busiest season.

Don't miss this opportunity to learn from organizations who are putting belonging and collaboration at the center of their work.

item.title: The Future of Job Search and Member Engagement: Panel Discussion for Associations
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Join our expert panel to explore how AI and workforce trends are reshaping job search & member engagement.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
As we head into 2026, associations face both opportunity and uncertainty. How will shifting budgets, evolving member expectations, and new workforce trends impact your ability to engage and retain members—while driving non-dues revenue?

Join our expert panel for a forward-looking discussion on the future of the job search and the role of the career center in your member engagement strategy. We’ll explore how AI and innovation are reshaping career development—and what that means for your members.

a:4:{s:6:"item-0";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:96:"Key trends to watch in 2026—from AI-driven recruiting to evolving jobs and association budgets";}s:6:"item-1";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:108:"How to position your association as the go-to career destination and strengthen year-round member engagement";}s:6:"item-2";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:93:"Ways to tailor career growth and learning opportunities to meet the needs of every generation";}s:6:"item-3";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:93:"Strategies to bridge the member–professional gap and unlock new sources of non-dues revenue";}}

item.title: Rethinking Retention: Generation-Specific Strategies to Address Lapser Risk and Boost Engagement
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover why Gen Z members are your highest lapser risk despite high satisfaction, and learn generation-specific strategies to boost retention during uncertain times.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn data-driven strategies to reduce lapser risk & boost retention across generations. Watch our webinar to learn more.
Rethinking Retention: Gen-Specific Strategies for Associations

From boomers to Gen Z, every generation engages differently — but they all want to belong. Join us as we decode member retention through the lens of TikToks, fax machines, and everything in between.

Most associations are still using one-size-fits-all approaches—and the data shows why that's costing you members.

Watch this on-demand webinar presents exclusive findings from Momentive's latest associations trends study, revealing critical insights about lapser risk across generations. While younger members report high engagement and satisfaction, they're also your highest flight risk. In times of economic and political uncertainty, understanding these generation-specific patterns is essential to maintaining your membership base.
In this data-driven session, you'll discover:

a:4:{s:6:"item-0";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:95:"The major differences in what drives renewal across Gen Z, Millennials, Gen X, and Baby Boomers";}s:6:"item-1";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:123:"Why Gen Z members are the riskiest members— your "shoppers" with the highest renewal intent but the lowest loyalty scores";}s:6:"item-2";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:84:"How to identify red flags for lapser risk among your most vulnerable member segments";}s:6:"item-3";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:100:"Generation-specific value offerings that transform satisfied members into loyal, long-term advocates";}}

Whether you're facing declining retention rates, trying to understand why younger members leave despite positive feedback, or looking to recession-proof your membership base, this webinar will equip you with actionable strategies to prioritize your highest-risk members.

Learn how to turn Gen Z "shoppers" into committed members and build generation-specific retention strategies that protect your association through uncertainty.

Don't miss this opportunity to gain data-backed insights that can transform your retention approach. Watch now!

Discover Path LMS Learning Paths in our webinar. Learn to build structured learning journeys for certification, compliance & growth.

Join us for an exclusive webinar introducing Learning Paths, a powerful new feature in Path LMS designed to create a predefined journey for certification, compliance, and professional development programs. Learning Paths help you guide learners through curated sequences of courses, assessments, and surveys - ensuring consistent outcomes and reducing administrative overhead.

item.title: Value That Pays Off: Expanding Revenue While Elevating Member Experience
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover how AMCs help associations increase revenue, optimize systems, and deliver greater member impact. Watch on-demand now!

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Expanding Revenue & Elevating Member Experience | Webinar
Learn strategies to grow association revenue, boost efficiency, and enhance member experience. Watch our expert-led webinar on-demand now!

a:4:{s:6:"item-0";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:0:"";s:14:"presenter_name";s:14:"Howard Pollock";s:21:"presenter_description";s:52:"Director of Enterprise Strategy, Momentive Software ";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-1";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:0:"";s:14:"presenter_name";s:19:"Rob Miller, MPA CAE";s:21:"presenter_description";s:49:"SVP of Associations Strategy, Momentive Software ";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-2";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:0:"";s:14:"presenter_name";s:12:"Mark Hopwood";s:21:"presenter_description";s:57:"Senior Director, Non-profit Strategy, Momentive Software ";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-3";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:0:"";s:14:"presenter_name";s:13:"Michael Kumpf";s:21:"presenter_description";s:41:"Director of Enterprise Sales, YM Careers ";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}}

As economic uncertainty and political volatility continue to strain association budgets, your clients are turning to you for guidance on how to do more with less — without compromising their mission. For many associations, declining member dues and mounting operational demands make long-term sustainability an increasing challenge. But with the right technology strategy and diversified revenue approach, there’s a path forward.  

In this session, we’ll explore how associations – and how AMCs can help clients – strengthen their financial footing by combining smart tech adoption with creative non-dues revenue strategies. From optimizing existing systems to building value-added member offerings, you’ll uncover practical ways to help associations increase revenue, boost efficiency, and deliver greater member impact — all without inflating budgets.

";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:13:"insight_title";s:35:"Doing More with Less — But Better";s:19:"insight_description";s:90:"How operational efficiencies, tech partnerships, and system optimization create capacity ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:13:"insight_title";s:30:"Technology as a Revenue Driver";s:19:"insight_description";s:170:"High-value member offerings that pay off, including professional development, CE programs, volunteer engagement, and more, to grow and diversify non-dues revenue streams ";}}

item.title: Set It and Forget It: The Donor Email System That Works While You Sleep
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Gain insights from simple automation techniques designed to extend your donor reach without adding to your daily tasks.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Gain insights from simple automation techniques designed to extend your donor reach without adding to your daily tasks.

Getting stuck in the endless cycle of donor emails? You're not alone.
Many nonprofit leaders juggle the challenges of staying engaged with donors while managing other responsibilities, often feeling like there's no escape from the constant demands of email communication.

Why join?

The session draws from real-world experiences, making it approachable for even the busiest nonprofits. It isn’t just about sending emails—it's about building lasting connections. We have tread this path before and are excited to share the insights that could reshape how you view email marketing. Rather than struggling to keep up, learn how to create a system where the work does itself, giving you more time to focus on the core mission of your nonprofit.
Reserve your spot today and find out how to turn data into relationships and relationships into lasting support. We’ll keep it practical, precise, and packed with actionable insights.

item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn how to leverage Google Grants for effective digital marketing & boost your nonprofit’s online visibility & donations.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
a:1:{s:6:"item-0";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"9713";s:14:"presenter_name";s:12:"Chris Barlow";s:21:"presenter_description";s:59:"Founder and Customer Happiness Director - Beeline Marketing";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:441:"

Beeline specializes in helping nonprofits grow their mission and donor base through digital marketing.

As Founder and Customer Happiness Director, Chris Barlow is grateful to help nonprofits use digital marketing to multiply the reach of their mission and the growth of their donors.

Most of all, he’s grateful to be the dad to seven kids, and to try to teach them how to live in a way that multiplies good in others.

";}}

GiveSmart and Beeline are combining forces for a marketing-themed webinar all about Google Grants for nonprofits and how they can help you grow your donor list every month in 2024.

What are Google Ad Grants? Most 501(c)3 organizations get $10,000 per month in FREE Search ads via Google.com.

If used effectively, these ads can help you grow your website traffic by thousands of people every month and add 50-200 new people to your email list every month.
In this session you will learn:

item.title: From One-Time to Lifetime: Turning Year-End Donors into Recurring Supporters
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Turn Year-End Donors into Recurring Supporters
Learn strategies to convert one-time year-end donors into loyal, recurring supporters & grow your nonprofit’s long-term fundraising.

The average recurring donor will give 42% more in one year than those who give one-time gifts.

The year-end giving season, including #GivingTuesday, is a crucial period for nonprofits, often attracting a surge of one-time donors eager to support a cause. However, the real opportunity lies in converting these donors into recurring supporters who provide sustained funding throughout the year.

In this webinar, we will guide you through proven strategies and actionable steps to nurture year-end donors into loyal, long-term supporters. Learn how to strengthen donor relationships, create compelling recurring giving programs, and leverage data-driven insights to enhance donor retention.

Increase the lifetime value of your donors while creating a more stable financial foundation for your nonprofit. Register now for an opportunity to strategize the future of donor relationships beyond individual giving moments.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Pathways to Success: Guided Learning Paths in Your LMS Webinar
Learn how learning paths turn your LMS into a guided experience. Watch our on-demand webinar on engagement & retention.

Stop losing learners. They’re seeking meaningful outcomes and continued engagement from the educational opportunities at your member organization. They should never have to ask, “What should I take next?”

Your LMS has the content. Help members navigate it. Creating structured learning paths within your LMS enables your staff to deliver what your members want, creating a truly strategic advantage and generating revenue for your organization.

In this webinar, you’ll learn practical approaches to designing learning paths that align with your learner types and that also deliver measurable impact.
By the end of this webinar, you’ll walk away with:

item.title: Turn Insights Into Impact: What 2025 Trends Mean for 2026 Strategy
item.dc:creator: colleen.bernum@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Join us for a data-driven webinar designed to help nonprofit organizations translate real-world fundraising trends into actionable strategies for the year ahead.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
What can your nonprofit learn from 2025 to fundraise smarter in 2026?

Join us for a data-driven webinar designed to help nonprofit organizations translate real-world fundraising trends into actionable strategies for the year ahead. Using nonprofit performance data and emerging sector trends, we’ll break down what’s working, what’s changing, and where nonprofits should focus in 2026 to maximize efficiency and impact.

item.title: Grow Sustainable Association Revenue With Proven eCommerce Strategies
item.dc:creator: colleen.bernum@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Watch our on-demand webinar to explore proven ecommerce strategies that help associations grow sustainable revenue through smarter digital commerce.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Watch our on-demand webinar to explore proven ecommerce strategies that help associations grow sustainable revenue through smarter digital commerce.
Ecommerce Strategies for Associations | On-Demand Webinar

Associations are under increasing pressure to diversify revenue beyond traditional dues as member expectations and digital demands continue to rise. One of the most effective ways to meet this challenge is through e-commerce bundling—a strategy long used by retailers to increase purchase value, streamline decision-making, and drive stronger engagement.

In this session, we’ll show how Nimble AMS makes it simple to utilize these proven bundling techniques to create more compelling offerings, boost non‑dues revenue, and deliver the digital experience members expect.

This webinar is ideal for associations looking for scalable, modern revenue strategies that deepen engagement and maximize average order value. Whether you're exploring product bundling for the first time or refining your current approach, you’ll walk away with practical insights you can put into action immediately—especially with seasonal buying peaks on the horizon.

item.dc:creator: colleen.bernum@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Personify clients are invited to a welcome webinar to learn about expanded possibilities, upcoming plans & opportunities with Momentive Software

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Momentive Software + Personify Client Welcome Webinar

Momentive Software recently welcomed Personify to its growing portfolio of intelligent solutions, bringing on better technology for nonprofits, associations, event organizers and mission-driven organizations.

By bringing together best-in-class platforms like A2Z Events, MemberClicks, ThreeSixty and CommUnity, Momentive is expanding the ways organizations manage memberships, events, learning, and engagement within a connected platform delivered by a single trusted partner.

Join members of Momentive’s leadership team to learn more about how Personify and Momentive are coming together and what it means for the solutions you already use today.

In this session, we’ll introduce the broader Momentive solutions, share the vision behind bringing these technologies together with Momentive IQ, a groundbreaking AI-powered platform, and highlight how the continued investment in innovation, data, and AI will help organizations streamline operations, strengthen engagement and deliver better experiences for members and event attendees.
Join us to learn what this exciting new chapter means for your organization and the solutions you use today.

item.title: The State of the Mission-Driven Workforce in 2026
item.dc:creator: colleen.bernum@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Explore new workforce research on burnout, retention & engagement across nonprofit and association teams. Learn how leaders can support & retain mission-driven talent.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Explore workforce research on burnout, retention & engagement across nonprofit & association teams. Support mission-driven talent.

a:2:{s:6:"item-0";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"9206";s:14:"presenter_name";s:14:"Nathan Richter";s:21:"presenter_description";s:35:"Senior Partner, Wakefield Research ";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-1";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"1690";s:14:"presenter_name";s:19:"Tirrah Switzer, CAE";s:21:"presenter_description";s:43:"VP of Product Marketing, Momentive Software";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}}

New research reveals what retains, burns out, and energizes the people behind mission-driven organizations

59% of mission-driven professionals are exploring other career opportunities. At the same time, 85% report feeling a strong sense of belonging at work.

For nonprofit and association leaders, these statistics signal a growing challenge: how to retain the people who power your mission while supporting their growth, engagement, and long-term impact.

Join Nathan Richter, Senior Partner at Wakefield Research, and Tirrah Switzer, CAE, VP of Product Marketing at Momentive Software, for a deeper dive into new research exploring the realities facing today’s mission-driven workforce.

Based on a national survey of nonprofit and association professionals, the State of the Mission-Driven Workforce in 2026 report examines the factors shaping employee engagement, burnout, career mobility, and optimism about the future of the sector.

In this session, we’ll explore what the data reveals about the experience of the people powering mission-driven organizations and what leaders can do to better support, engage, and retain their teams.
This webinar is part of a four-part series. Register now for the first part!
Fill out the form to save your spot

The State of the Mission-Driven Workforce in 2026 report was conducted by Wakefield Research in partnership with Momentive Software.

The study surveyed 500 nonprofit and association professionals across the United States, each with management-level seniority or higher and representing organizations with at least $500K in annual revenue.

The research explores the workforce dynamics shaping mission-driven organizations today, including career development, technology experiences, skills gaps, workplace culture, and long-term workforce optimism.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: GiveSmart On-Demand Webinar | Data Insights Every Nonprofit Should Use

Watch now to learn how your organization can make smarter, more confident decisions using data and analytics. In this session, we’ll break down what nonprofits should actually be measuring, how to spot meaningful patterns, and how to use data to predict outcomes, plan strategically, and perform more effectively.

item.title: Investing in Your People: How Professional Development Drives Staff Retention in Associations and Nonprofits
item.dc:creator: colleen.bernum@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn professional development strategies that help associations & organizations improve staff retention. Join this webinar to discover workforce growth solutions.​

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Professional Development Strategies for Staff Retention​
professional development strategies,nonprofit staff retention,staff retention strategies,workforce development strategies,career development programs

If you've ever lost a great staff member, you know the real cost goes far beyond posting a job listing. Recruiting, onboarding, the learning curve, the institutional knowledge that walks out the door — for associations and nonprofits, where budgets are already stretched, turnover can hit especially hard.

One of the most effective ways to hold onto talented people is also one of the most underutilized: investing in their growth.

In this webinar, we'll explore what staff turnover actually costs your organization — and how building real professional development opportunities can drastically improve retention. When employees see a clear path to grow, build new skills, and advance their careers, they're far more likely to stay engaged and committed to the mission.

You'll leave with practical strategies for creating meaningful development opportunities, even on a limited budget, that strengthen your team, improve performance, and protect your organization's most valuable resources — its people.
Join us to discover how prioritizing your people's growth helps your organization operate more efficiently, more sustainably, and with greater mission impact.

The research explores the workforce dynamics shaping mission-driven organizations today, including career development, technology experiences, skills gaps, workplace culture, and long-term workforce optimism.

item.title: The Retention Chain: How Supporting Staff Strengthens Donor and Volunteer Loyalty
item.dc:creator: colleen.bernum@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn staff retention strategies that boost donor and volunteer loyalty. Practical insights for associations and mission-driven organizations.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: a:2:{s:6:"item-0";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"9170";s:14:"presenter_name";s:12:"Mark Hopwood";s:21:"presenter_description";s:55:"Senior Director, Nonprofit Strategy, Momentive Software";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-1";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"9291";s:14:"presenter_name";s:11:"Kate McGinn";s:21:"presenter_description";s:34:"Founder & CEO, The Bold Stripe";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}}

Retention is one of the biggest challenges facing nonprofit organizations today. But donor and volunteer loyalty doesn’t exist in a vacuum—it’s deeply connected to the experience of the staff who support them.

Join nonprofit strategy leaders Kate McGinn and Mark Hopwood for an authentic conversation that explores how staff retention directly influences donor and volunteer retention.

Drawing on insights from Momentive’s 2026 State of the Mission-Driven Workforce Report, this session will examine the workforce realities shaping nonprofit organizations today. While 85% of professionals report strong workplace belonging, 59% are exploring other opportunities, revealing a critical challenge for mission-driven organizations that depend on stable relationships to sustain impact.

In this conversation, Kate and Mark will connect the dots between staff experience, volunteer engagement, and donor loyalty, sharing practical strategies to strengthen retention across all three.

You’ll learn how organizations can create the conditions where staff thrive, so the communities supporting your mission stay engaged for the long term.

If you’re focused on strengthening donor relationships, building a more engaged volunteer community, or improving staff retention, this session will provide actionable insights you can apply right away.

item.title: Why Staff Retention Is Your New Member Retention Strategy
item.dc:creator: colleen.bernum@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover proven member retention strategies to reduce churn, strengthen staff experience & improve association membership retention.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Member Retention Strategies for Stronger Associations
member retention strategies,association membership retention,member engagement strategies,membership retention best practices

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"9863";s:14:"presenter_name";s:30:"Kerri McGovern, MPP, CAE, AAiP";s:21:"presenter_description";s:100:"VP Membership, Engagement, and Volunteerism, Council for Advancement and Support of Education (CASE)";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-1";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:5:"10473";s:14:"presenter_name";s:20:"Teshieka Curtis-Pugh";s:21:"presenter_description";s:53:"Executive Director, South Carolina Nurses Association";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-2";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"1935";s:14:"presenter_name";s:20:"Rob Miller, MPA, CAE";s:21:"presenter_description";s:47:"SVP of Association Strategy, Momentive Software";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}}

Tech burnout from disconnected systems may seem like a strictly internal issue, but recent research shows that those effects bleed into your membership and ultimately lead to churn.

Inefficient, disconnected tech is burning out association staff — and when staff struggle, member experience suffers. In this session, Kerri McGovern of CASE, Teshieka Curtis-Pugh of the South Carolina Nurses Association, and Rob Miller of Momentive Software dig into why staff retention is your most powerful member‑retention strategy and a critical driver of long‑term association sustainability.
Walk away with a roadmap to reduce burnout, support your team, and ultimately create a stronger, more sustainable member experience.

item.title: AMS implementation made easy: Prepare your small association for tech transformation
item.dc:creator: colleen.bernum@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Watch this 15-minute on-demand webinar to discover best practices to prepare your small staff association for a successful AMS implementation!

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: AMS Implementation for Small Staff Associations | Webinar

How technologically prepared does your association feel for the future? According to the Small Staff Benchmark Report, just 5% of small associations feel very technologically prepared to meet evolving member needs.

However, getting your association up to speed with technology that meets both the needs of staff and members is easier than you think. The right software solution can help you increase membership, improve retention, and offer greater member value. And with a few simple change management strategies, your small staff can transition smoothly.

Watch this 15-minute on-demand webinar to discover best practices to prepare your small staff association for a successful AMS implementation!

item.dc:creator: colleen.bernum@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Watch our on-demand webinar: Improve Member Loyalty with the Perfect AMS Fit and kickstart your journey toward upgrading your technology to elevate member loyalty and engagement.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Navigating AMS Selection and Implementation with Confidence

Is your organization exploring new technology solutions this year? You’re not alone! Deloitte research shows that most U.S. organizations have increased their digital transformation budgets since 2020.

With tighter budgets, member-based organizations must make every tech investment count. The good news? Finding the right membership management software can drive cost efficiency and innovation at your organization.

Watch this webinar for expert tips to streamline your technology decision-making process and ensure a successful AMS implementation.

item.title: Mapping success: An AMS implementation roadmap for mid-to-large associations
item.dc:creator: colleen.bernum@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Watch our webinar to discover best practices to prepare your association staff for a successful AMS implementation!

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: AMS Implementation Roadmap for Mid-to-Large Associations

Is your organization preparing to implement new technology? You might find the current process challenging—and if so, you’re not alone! According to the latest Association Trends Study, association professionals report implementing new software as one of their greatest technological challenges.

While choosing the right software, successfully implementing, and launching your new platform might seem daunting, with the right resources, you can set the stage for successful software implementation. And, when you effectively adopt new technology, you’ll enhance both the staff and member experiences, putting you on track to reach your larger organizational goals.

Watch our webinar to discover best practices to prepare your association staff for a successful AMS implementation!

item.title: Maximize your AMS ROI: How to drive long-term success
item.dc:creator: colleen.bernum@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Watch our webinar to discover best practices to optimize your AMS investment and drive long-term value.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Maximize your AMS ROI: How to drive long-term success

Congratulations—you’ve done the hard work of researching next your association management software! Now it’s time to reap the rewards.

But how can your organization maximize its AMS investment far into the future? Take the first step to success by building a partnership with your AMS vendor. When you foster a collaborative relationship with your software provider, you’ll meet your future organizational goals and technological needs. Work with your AMS vendor to continually optimize your technology through product updates, feature enhancements, and innovative offerings.

You can begin investing in your AMS today! Watch our on-demand webinar to discover best practices to optimize your AMS investment and drive long-term value.

item.title: Sustainable Fundraising Starts Within: Retaining Talent, Volunteers, and Donor Revenue
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn how staff and volunteer engagement directly impacts donor retention, recurring giving, and long‑term fundraising revenue.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Sustainable Fundraising | Retain Staff, Volunteers, and Funds
Learn how staff and volunteer engagement directly impacts donor retention, recurring giving, and long‑term fundraising revenue.

a:2:{s:6:"item-0";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:5:"10474";s:14:"presenter_name";s:10:"Grace Dick";s:21:"presenter_description";s:102:"Partnerships Manager, Double the Donation";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:614:"

Grace Dick is a philanthropic technology strategist dedicated to modernizing how nonprofits capture revenue. As the Partnerships Manager at Double the Donation, Grace spearheads collaborations at the intersection of corporate philanthropy and social impact, empowering more than 8,000 leading organizations to diversify their funding streams. Grace believes deeply that technology should make sophisticated revenue strategies accessible to organizations of all sizes. Grace leverages her background in fundraising and community outreach to connect nonprofit professionals with tools to scale their impact.

";}s:6:"item-1";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:5:"10596";s:14:"presenter_name";s:15:"Chelsey Wilson ";s:21:"presenter_description";s:51:"Senior Product Marketing Manager Momentive Software";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:695:"

Chelsey brings a perspective most product marketers don't have — she's actually lived the challenges our customers face. With a background spanning nonprofit leadership, marketing, and event management, she understands firsthand what it takes to run a mission-driven organization.

Now, as a Senior Product Marketing Manager at Momentive Software, she channels that experience into market research, industry analysis, and strategic positioning that keeps our solutions aligned with where the nonprofit and association space is heading. It's the through-line of her career: connecting what organizations need with what technology can do to help them thrive.

";}}

Fundraisers know the challenge: acquiring new donors is expensive, and keeping them is getting harder.

In this webinar, we’ll break down insights from Momentive's recent mission‑driven trends research to show how staff, volunteer, and donor retention directly affect fundraising revenue and long-term sustainability. You’ll learn where fundraising streams are most at risk due to poor retention, and what nonprofit professionals can do to protect these revenue streams going forward.

item.title: Your LMS Is Not a Library. It's a Revenue Engine: Association Leaders Share What's Actually Working
item.dc:creator: colleen.bernum@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Join this ASAE webinar to explore personalized learning paths, certifications, and cohort programs that grow association LMS revenue.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Association Leaders on What's Working in LMS Strategy
Join this ASAE webinar to explore personalized learning paths, certifications, and cohort programs that grow association LMS revenue.

What if your Learning Management System (LMS) isn’t just a digital filing cabinet, storing webinars and other content, but an engagement engine and a source of non-dues revenue?

In this ASAE complimentary webinar, sponsored by Momentive, a panel of association executives explores how they use their LMS to move beyond static content libraries and create ongoing member value. Panelists discuss how personalized learning paths, certifications, cohort‑based programs, and data‑driven insights can increase engagement between events, strengthen retention, and unlock new revenue streams through premium courses, sponsorship opportunities, and bundled offerings.

You walk away with practical strategies to reposition your LMS from a back‑office system to a forward‑facing growth driver—one that deepens community, fuels career advancement, and contributes meaningfully to your organization’s financial sustainability.

Looking to streamline your fundraising, engage more donors, and grow your impact with a solution that drives 20x ROI in one year?
Join us for a look at GiveSmart, the all-in-one fundraising, events, and donor management platform built to help organizations like yours do more by paying for less. In this 45-minute backstage look at the platform, you’ll discover how GiveSmart’s award-winning tools can run successful fundraising campaigns, manage donors effortlessly, and help you raise more money year-round.
Whether you’re focused on auctions, donor stewardship, peer-to-peer fundraising, or automated donation options, this demo will show you how GiveSmart supports your goals and scales with your mission.

item.title: From Silos to Sync: Modernizing Continuing Education
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Join our live webinar to learn how modernizing continuing education breaks down silos, improves integration, and enhances learner engagement.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
Managing continuing education shouldn’t mean duplicate data entry or disconnected systems. With the latest Catalog Sync upgrade, associations can create and manage CE programs in Path LMS while course data automatically syncs to YourMembership AMS—along with orders, credits, and member access via SSO. The result: less manual work, greater accuracy, and a more cohesive experience for staff and learners alike. 

Watch this webinar to see how smarter CE delivery improves the member experience, increases operational efficiency, and supports sustainable revenue growth.

item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Watch this webinar to learn how YourMembership AMS + YM Careers increase member value, engagement, and non-dues revenue. Demo included.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Grow Revenue with Member Benefits | On-Demand Webinar
Watch this webinar to learn how YourMembership AMS + YM Careers increase member value, engagement, and non-dues revenue. Demo included.
Valuable Member Benefits That Grow Revenue

Associations are facing rising member expectations and increasing pressure to deliver clear value—especially as more members pay their own dues and reassess what they’re getting in return. Career support is one of the most impactful benefits you can offer: 41% of members rank career advancement as their top benefit, and 46% have used their organization’s job board or career center.  

In this webinar, you’ll learn how YourMembership AMS + YM Careers work together to deliver those high‑value benefits and unlock new revenue streams to strengthen long‑term sustainability.

item.title: Beyond Bids: Best Practices and Proven Strategies to Perfecting the Live Auction
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn expert tips and strategies to run successful live auctions that maximize bids and donor engagement in this practical webinar.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Live Auction Best Practices & Proven Strategies
Learn expert tips and strategies to run successful live auctions that maximize bids and donor engagement in this practical webinar.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"9656";s:14:"presenter_name";s:16:"Dan Campbell BAS";s:21:"presenter_description";s:54:"AMM Raising Paddles Benefit Auctions & Fundraising";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:1146:"

Dan Campbell is a professional fundraising auctioneer based in St Charles, IL. Dan is one of approximately 300 Benefit Auction Specialists, a certification that identifies those auctioneers who specialize in all aspects of fundraising. Dan has served hundreds of nonprofits, and helped raise over $250 million over his career for various causes and schools.

Raising Paddles is one of the largest fundraising teams in the United States, helping 300 nonprofits raise more than $80 million annually. Raising Paddles primarily serves the Midwest, but also supports key clients around the country. Raising Paddles provides educational workshops throughout the year in various cities providing subject matter expertise on fundraising events. In addition, Raising Paddles is a consulting firm that provides year-round consulting to clients on board training, capital campaigns, event planning, and donor development strategies.

";}s:6:"item-1";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"9657";s:14:"presenter_name";s:16:"Madison Kautman ";s:21:"presenter_description";s:33:"Senior Campaign Manager GiveSmart";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:490:"

Madison Kautman is the Senior Campaign Manager for GiveSmart and a fundraising professional with a passion for the arts and culture sector. Before joining the GiveSmart team, Madison worked within many nonprofit organizations including art galleries, art museums, children’s museums, and a healthcare clinic. In addition, she was a GiveSmart customer for about two years, utilizing the platform to facilitate many events and fundraisers to make a difference in her community. ​

";}s:6:"item-2";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"9658";s:14:"presenter_name";s:12:"Tara Kielty ";s:21:"presenter_description";s:26:"Campaign Manager GiveSmart";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}}

Join GiveSmart and Raising Paddles for a best practices webinar to boost your auction strategies in 2024!

In this comprehensive session, we’ll guide you through the essential elements of creating a successful live auction, offering best practices on how to deliver maximum results during your fundraising program. We will discuss the principles of designing a live auction lineup, best practices for promoting your items before and during your event, and discover ideas on how the live auction can impact the rest of your fundraising elements, such as the silent auction, raffles, and even the paddle raise.
