item.title: Calling for Action: How to Creatively Engage Donors for a Successful Giving Tuesday
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn creative strategies to engage donors & maximize impact this Giving Tuesday with actionable tips for nonprofits.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Engage Donors for a Successful Giving Tuesday
Learn creative strategies to engage donors & maximize impact this Giving Tuesday with actionable tips for nonprofits.

a:2:{s:6:"item-0";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"9674";s:14:"presenter_name";s:15:"Ashlee Droscher";s:21:"presenter_description";s:57:"CFRE Director of Development Radiation Oncology Institute";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:1706:"

Ashlee Droscher, CFRE, is a nonprofit executive with more than two decades of experience helping organizations tell their story and connect with donors. Currently, she serves as Director of Development for the Radiation Oncology Institute (ROI), the 501(c)(3) foundation of the American Society for Radiation Oncology (ASTRO), where she leads revenue strategy, events, and brand presence—broadening the individual and family-foundation pipeline, refreshing donor communications and brand guidelines, and revitalizing signature experiences.

Previously, Ashlee was Director of Development at the SHRM Foundation, stewarding an annual campaign of roughly $5 million, securing multiple six-figure grants and sponsorships, and designing national initiatives that mobilized employers to support veterans, individuals with disabilities, and people with records. As Development Director for the Animal Welfare League of Alexandria, she led 75th-anniversary campaigns and events that expanded monthly giving by 48% and grew overall revenue 12% year over year.

Earlier in her career, Ashlee held progressively senior roles with the National Multiple Sclerosis Society, leading campaigns exceeding $2.3 million annually and consulting across the Society’s 50-state network. She began in public relations and events with the American Red Cross. Ashlee holds an M.S. in Organization Development (American University/NTL) and a B.A. in Communication, Public Relations (University of the Pacific), and has been a Certified Fund Raising Executive since 2014. In her spare time, Ashlee volunteers in her community, walks her very slow English bulldog, and tries to keep up with her two young kids.

";}s:6:"item-1";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"9675";s:14:"presenter_name";s:16:"Madison Kautman ";s:21:"presenter_description";s:73:"Senior Marketing Campaign Manager GiveSmart Powered by Momentive Software";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:1342:"

Madison Kautman joined the GiveSmart team over four years ago and is a fundraising professional with over eight years of experience working with nonprofits. Before her time with GiveSmart, Madison worked with numerous nonprofit organizations, including art galleries, art museums, children’s museums, and healthcare clinics. Through her nonprofit work, she spent about two years as a GiveSmart customer and has extensive experience utilizing the platform to facilitate events and fundraisers. ​At her core, Madison is someone who loves to work for a mission that makes a measurable impact. In her current role, she is passionate about helping nonprofits and providing them with the resources they need to continue impacting our communities every day.

";}}

Ready to make this Giving Tuesday your most impactful yet? This webinar is designed to help nonprofit professionals craft mission-driven calls to action that resonate with, motivate, and drive donor action this Giving Tuesday.

We’ll explore how to position your mission in a way that captures hearts, turns your mission into clear and confident storytelling, and builds urgency without pressuring donors.
Whether you’re new to Giving Tuesday or looking to refine your strategy, this session will give you actionable insights and real-world examples to help you:

Don’t miss your chance to energize your audience, elevate your storytelling, and make this Giving Tuesday your strongest yet. Watch now!

item.title: Introducing MomentiveIQ + GiveSmart
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Register now to see GiveSmart’s new data analytics with MomentiveIQ and see what’s possible for the future of your fundraising—powered by AI.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: MomentiveIQ AI Analytics for GiveSmart | Smarter Fundraising
Discover how the new MomentiveIQ AI Analytics for GiveSmart helps nonprofits unify data, improve accuracy, & use AI to instantly uncover insights.

Transform Fragmented Data into Fundraising Clarity with Built-In AI Tools

Join us for the official launch of MomentiveIQ AI Analytics for GiveSmart, purpose-built to help mission-driven organizations work smarter with their fundraising data. 

Nonprofits often struggle with disconnected systems, manual reporting that requires stitching together, and inconsistent data that slow decision-making and limit insight. With the new MomentiveIQ AI Analytics for GiveSmart, your data can be automatically unified, validated, and ready for action — no spreadsheets or technical expertise required.
In this webinar, you’ll see how AI-powered Analytics will enable you to:

a:5:{s:6:"item-0";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:50:"Eliminate manual data pulls and cobbling together ";}s:6:"item-1";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:45:"Improve data accuracy and trust across teams ";}s:6:"item-2";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:65:"See near real-time data visualizations with drill-down reporting ";}s:6:"item-3";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:75:"Quickly uncover donor trends, performance drivers, and strategic takeaways ";}s:6:"item-4";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:10:"And more! ";}}

Whether you’re preparing for your next campaign, forecasting revenue, or reporting to leadership, MomentiveIQ makes it easier to turn GiveSmart data into clear answers — so you can spend less time managing data and more time advancing your mission.

a:2:{s:6:"item-0";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"9799";s:14:"presenter_name";s:15:"Tirrah Switzer ";s:21:"presenter_description";s:49:"President of Product Marketing Momentive Software";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:618:"

Tirrah is the Vice President of Product Marketing with Momentive Software. She is the co-author of 11 research studies on nonprofit and event trends, member engagement and loyalty, and association digital evolution trends.

";}s:6:"item-1";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"9675";s:14:"presenter_name";s:16:"Madison Kautman ";s:21:"presenter_description";s:73:"Senior Marketing Campaign Manager GiveSmart Powered by Momentive Software";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:1064:"

Before joining the GiveSmart team, Madison worked within many nonprofit organizations including art galleries, art museums, children’s museums, and children’s healthcare. She enjoys providing nonprofits with the tools they need to be successful, further their missions, and continue impacting our communities.

";}}

Data shows that 63% of nonprofit professionals are optimistic about the next 12 months. In the midst of today’s unpredictable funding landscape, how are the most resilient nonprofits not only surviving—but thriving—and remaining optimistic?

Join GiveSmart for an insightful webinar that unpacks the findings of Momentive Software’s recently published study, Weathering Federal Uncertainty: Nonprofit Operational Trends, which reveals how top-performing organizations are growing revenue through flexibility and future-focused strategies. Unpack actionable takeaways based on the new data that your organization can use moving forward through 2025 and beyond.

As Senior Product Marketing Manager for GiveSmart, Chelsey is driven to understand the unique challenges and needs of the modern nonprofit organization and is excited to contribute to a team that is creating solutions that empower mission-driven organizations. Chelsey loves diving into market research and competitive sleuthing to uncover the latest trends and strategies that will help organizations thrive. Her background in the nonprofit space, as well as marketing and event management, gives her perspective and insight to apply research to real-world scenarios. This serves Chelsey’s passion for supporting nonprofits and the missions that help our communities thrive.

";}}

Data shows—every generation gives differently. Does your nonprofit’s strategy reflect that?

Understanding donor behavior across generations is key to unlocking new revenue opportunities and strengthening donor relationships. In this webinar, we’ll break down the latest giving trends from Boomers to Gen Z, helping you optimize your fundraising approach to maximize support across all age groups.

item.title: Challenge Accepted: Using Competition to Elevate Your Fundraising
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn how using competition can motivate donors, increase engagement, & elevate your fundraising results in this actionable webinar.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn how using competition can motivate donors, increase engagement, & elevate your fundraising results in this actionable webinar.

a:2:{s:6:"item-0";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"9815";s:14:"presenter_name";s:12:"Kate McGinn ";s:21:"presenter_description";s:39:"Chief Executive Officer The Bold Stripe";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:1011:"

With more than 10 years of experience driving transformational results in the non-profit sector, Kate McGinn has spent her career championing the impact of investing in your organization’s leaders – both volunteer and staff.

As the CEO & Founder of The Bold Stripe, a nonprofit consulting company, she spends her days helping organizations build capacity by investing in great strategy, top-tier training and coaching, and smart organizational and fundraising campaign design – all to maximize the power of individuals to drive impact.

Prior to The Bold Stripe, Kate spent the largest chunk of her career at The Leukemia & Lymphoma Society, overseeing budgets of up to $85M+. As a former National VP, she has experience leading two of the top ten Peer-to-Peer campaigns in the country – Visionaries of the Year and Student Visionaries of the Year – and she’s worked with volunteer leaders who have raised anywhere from $50,000 to $1M+ individually in just a few months.

";}s:6:"item-1";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"9675";s:14:"presenter_name";s:16:"Madison Kautman ";s:21:"presenter_description";s:65:"Senior Marketing Campaign Manager GiveSmart by Momentive Software";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:1065:"

Before joining the GiveSmart team, Madison worked within many nonprofit organizations including art galleries, art museums, children’s museums, and a healthcare clinic. ";}}

Looking to energize your campaigns or events and inspire donors to give more? Discover how to add excitement, increase donor engagement, and create memorable experiences that drive results.

This webinar will explore how nonprofits like yours can harness the power of gamification and friendly competition to supercharge fundraising efforts. From interactive games and leaderboards to peer-to-peer challenges and prize-driven incentives, you’ll learn creative ways to make fundraising fun and effective.

Whether you’re hosting a gala, golf outing, peer-to-peer event or online campaign, you’ll walk away with actionable ideas and real-world examples that turn fundraising into a game everyone wants to play. Ready, set, raise!

item.title: Giving Tuesday Game Plan: Strategic Marketing for Nonprofits
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn actionable strategies to plan & execute a successful Giving Tuesday campaign that drives donations & engages supporters.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Giving Tuesday Marketing Strategies for Nonprofits
Learn actionable strategies to plan & execute a successful Giving Tuesday campaign that drives donations & engages supporters.

a:2:{s:6:"item-0";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"9820";s:14:"presenter_name";s:17:"Andrea Genevieve ";s:21:"presenter_description";s:25:"Andrea Genevieve Creative";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:779:"

Andrea Genevieve is a seasoned marketing consultant with 17+ years of experience in digital marketing, specializing in social media. Her strategic approach and creative flair have earned her a reputation for delivering innovative solutions tailored to diverse client needs, from startups to Fortune 500 companies and nonprofit organizations. Andrea is a thought leader in the industry, sharing expertise as a frequent speaker and adjunct professor. She often serves as a trusted advisor for organizations aiming to make a lasting impact. Andrea lives in Asheville, NC with her husband and three children. An avid traveler, when she’s not creating content or consulting nonprofits, you’ll find her outdoors exploring waterfalls and enjoying road trips with her family.

She has spearheaded marketing and fundraising programs, and developed business partnerships to support organizational growth for a nonprofit afterschool program. ";}}

Get ready to tackle Giving Tuesday marketing like a pro! Join us for a dynamic and fun-filled webinar that’s all about crafting the ultimate game plan for your nonprofit’s Giving Tuesday campaign.

Whether you’re the quarterback of your development team or a key player on the social media squad, this session will equip you with the winning strategies you need to score big on the biggest giving day of the year.

We’ll cover everything from setting clear goals to crafting compelling messages, leveraging social media, and engaging with your donor base in a meaningful way. Attendees will learn how to design a strategic marketing plan that aligns with their mission, drives donor engagement, and maximizes impact.

item.excerpt:encoded: Discover proven strategies to plan & execute successful golf fundraising events that drive engagement & boost donations.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Discover proven strategies to plan & execute successful golf fundraising events that drive engagement & boost donations.

a:2:{s:6:"item-0";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"9823";s:14:"presenter_name";s:11:"Dana Smith ";s:21:"presenter_description";s:41:"Senior Customer Success Manager GiveSmart";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:415:"

Learning how to host a successful golf fundraising event is par for the course. Join the GiveSmart experts as they guide you through the essential strategies and insights to elevate your fundraising efforts on the greens. item.title: Winning Bids: Maximize Auction Revenue with Data-Driven Item Selection
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn how to use data to select auction items that drive higher bids & boost fundraising revenue for your nonprofit events.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Maximize Auction Revenue with Data-Driven Items
Learn how to use data to select auction items that drive higher bids & boost fundraising revenue for your nonprofit events.

What auction items are actually bringing in the most dollars for nonprofits, and which ones are falling flat?

Join us for this data-packed session to discover how real auction insights can help you select better items, increase engagement, and raise more revenue at your next fundraising event. Whether you’re planning a gala, golf outing, or online auction, we’ll share the top-performing item categories, bidding trends, and strategies to help you make smarter choices and avoid guesswork.

item.title: Nimble AMS Summer ‘26 Release Overview Webinar
item.dc:creator: colleen.bernum@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
As summer kicks off, we’re excited to introduce the Nimble AMS Summer ’26 release, delivering meaningful updates that modernize the member experience, improve financial flexibility, and reduce manual work for your team. In this live webinar, our product experts will walk through what’s new, why it matters, and how you can take advantage of these improvements. 

Join us for a guided walkthrough of the Summer ’26 release, featuring enhancements designed to streamline operations, improve self‑service for members, and support more flexible business and financial workflows.
This release reflects direct customer feedback and focuses on foundational improvements across the Member Portal, payments, billing, and events. During the session, we’ll cover:

a:6:{s:6:"item-0";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:212:"Personal Snapshot with Join & Renew – Give members a modern, self‑service view of their profile, affiliations, and membership status, with flexible join and renewal flows directly in the Member Portal.";}s:6:"item-1";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:154:"Template Viewer – Render existing Nimble Create templates inside the Member Portal, preserving prior investments while expanding portal adoption.";}s:6:"item-2";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:141:"Payment Processing Fees (Beta) – Recover payment processing costs with configurable flat or percentage‑based fees by payment type.";}s:6:"item-3";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:137:"Split Revenue – Distribute revenue from a single transaction across multiple general ledger accounts without custom development.";}s:6:"item-4";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:123:"Bulk Billing (Beta) – Create and manage membership and subscription billing at scale using updated UOA workflows. ";}s:6:"item-5";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:156:"Member Portal Event Registration (Beta) – Deliver a modern, configurable event registration experience for main registrants, guests, and coworkers.";}}
Don’t miss your opportunity to see these updates in action and understand what’s available now and what’s coming next.

item.title: The Shift: How Associations and Nonprofits Are Adapting to What's Next
item.dc:creator: colleen.bernum@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: This four-part series draws on original research from three separate studies of association leaders, nonprofit executives, and association members. Each session tackles similar pressure points across different themes: generational change, AI adoption, revenue sustainability, and donor trust.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: The Shift: How Associations and Nonprofits Are Adapting to What's Next

This four-part series draws on original research from three separate studies of association leaders, nonprofit executives, and association members. Each session tackles similar pressure points across different themes: generational change, AI adoption, revenue sustainability, and donor trust.

a:9:{s:6:"item-0";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:0:"";s:14:"presenter_name";s:22:"Mary Connor, CAE, AAiP";s:21:"presenter_description";s:53:"Chief Strategy Officer, Stringfellow Management Group";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-1";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:0:"";s:14:"presenter_name";s:13:"Dustin Radtke";s:21:"presenter_description";s:36:"Chief AI Officer, Momentive Software";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-2";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:0:"";s:14:"presenter_name";s:30:"Rich Vallaster, CEM, QAS, AAiP";s:21:"presenter_description";s:54:" Sr. Director of Industry Strategy, Momentive Software";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-3";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:0:"";s:14:"presenter_name";s:14:"Patrick Gotham";s:21:"presenter_description";s:39:" Senior Vice President, CCS Fundraising";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-4";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:0:"";s:14:"presenter_name";s:19:"Tirrah Switzer, CAE";s:21:"presenter_description";s:53:"Vice President, Product Marketing, Momentive Software";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-5";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:0:"";s:14:"presenter_name";s:15:"Chris Capistran";s:21:"presenter_description";s:53:"Vice President, Industry Strategy, Momentive Software";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-6";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:0:"";s:14:"presenter_name";s:14:"Chelsey Wilson";s:21:"presenter_description";s:52:"Senior Product Marketing Manager, Momentive Software";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-7";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:0:"";s:14:"presenter_name";s:17:"Michelle Baughman";s:21:"presenter_description";s:74:"Manager, Exhibits and Sponsorship, America Society of Landscape Architects";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-8";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:0:"";s:14:"presenter_name";s:13:"Daniel Martin";s:21:"presenter_description";s:72:"Managing Director, Development, American Society of Landscape Architects";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}}

Strategies for Organizations Navigating Generational and Technological Change

Your membership pipeline looks different than it did three years ago. So does your donor base, your staff's relationship with technology, and the revenue model you've relied on for decades. You already know something fundamental is changing. The question is what to do about it.

The organizations moving forward have realized technology has already far surpassed where anyone predicted, and that industry leaders must adapt to change without getting left behind. These leaders are having honest conversations with their organizations about the gap between where they are and where they need to be—and closing it with intention.

This four-part series draws on original research from three separate studies of association leaders, nonprofit executives, and association members. Each session tackles similar pressure points across different themes: generational change, AI adoption, revenue sustainability, and donor trust. Together, they give you a grounded picture of what's shifting and a practical path forward.
Fill out the form to save your spot
This series has the following sessions:

a:4:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:13:"insight_title";s:101:" Session 1: AI Is Moving Fast. Are Your People Moving With It?
July 21 | 2:00 PM ET";s:19:"insight_description";s:399:"Your staff want to adapt, and research shows they're actively asking for AI education. The gap isn't apathy; it's that most organizations haven't built the structures or culture to meet that demand. Join Mary Connor, Dustin Radtke, and Rich Vallastar as they pull back the curtain on what AI change management actually looks like for mission-driven organizations and executives leading the strategy.";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:13:"insight_title";s:129:" Session 2: The Trust Economy: Why Donor Trust Is Harder to Earn—and How to Win It Back
July 22 | 12:00 PM ET";s:19:"insight_description";s:356:"89% of nonprofit executives say donor trust is harder to earn today than three years ago, and it now ranks as the #1 organizational priority for the year ahead. Drawing on new research from 500 U.S. nonprofit executives, Patrick Gotham and Tirrah Switzer map exactly where trust is breaking down and what the organizations earning it are doing differently.";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:13:"insight_title";s:140:"Session 3: When Dues Aren't Enough: Revenue Strategies for Associations Facing a Changing Membership
July 23 | 12:00 PM ET";s:19:"insight_description";s:412:"62% of members now pay their own dues, and cost remains the #1 reason they leave. A growing gap between what associations offer and what members value is putting long-term revenue at risk, but this moment also presents a real opportunity for organizations willing to adapt their model. Join Chris Capistran and Chelsey Wilson as they reveal research and strategies that will help you reinvigorate member loyalty.";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:13:"insight_title";s:125:"Session 4: Fill the Pipeline: What Gen Z and Millennials Actually Need From Your Event
July 23 | 2:00 PM ET";s:19:"insight_description";s:319:"By the end of 2026, Millennials and Gen Z will make up nearly three-quarters of the workforce—yet most associations struggle to convert younger attendees into long-term members. Guided by Michelle Baughman, Daniel Martin, and Rich Vallastar, learn what works, what doesn't, and how to apply it before your next event.";}}

item.title: Unlocking Major Gifts: How to Identify Your Largest Donors
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn strategies to identify & engage your largest donors to secure major gifts & grow your nonprofit’s fundraising impact.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Unlock Major Gifts: Identify Top Donors
Learn strategies to identify & engage your largest donors to secure major gifts & grow your nonprofit’s fundraising impact.

a:2:{s:6:"item-0";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"9672";s:14:"presenter_name";s:13:"Pooya Pourak ";s:21:"presenter_description";s:25:"Co-Founder, CEO MatchNice";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:1178:"

Pooya Pourak is the Co-Founder and CEO of MatchNice, where his mission is to inspire more giving by making donation matching easy, transparent, and accessible.

With a foundation in industrial engineering, data analytics, and human-centered design, Pooya has consulted across the for-profit, government, and nonprofit sectors, applying systems thinking to drive social impact. As the host of The Nonprofit Lab podcast, he challenges the status quo by spotlighting bold ideas that reshape generosity and nonprofit innovation. He also serves as the research and analytics consultant for The Nonprofit Alliance, where he drives insights and supports advocacy to protect the nonprofit sector amid today’s complex political landscape. Outside of work, Pooya is an Ironman triathlete, dog lover, avid hiker, and certified yoga instructor—bringing presence, vulnerability, and empathy to everything he does.

Pooya holds a B.S. in Industrial and Systems Engineering from Georgia Institute of Technology.

";}s:6:"item-1";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"9837";s:14:"presenter_name";s:11:"Lisa Greer ";s:21:"presenter_description";s:57:"Bestselling Author, Philanthropist, and Fundraising Coach";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:1742:"

Lisa Zola Greer is a philanthropist, nonprofit advisor, convener, and bestselling author of Philanthropy Revolution and The Essential Fundraiser’s Handbook. A relentless advocate for transforming fundraising, Lisa has trained and spoken to thousands of fundraisers worldwide, including groups such as AFP, CASE, APRA, Philanthropy Ireland, Fundraising Everywhere, Jewish Funder’s Network and the UK Chartered Institute of Fundraising – challenging outdated practices and pushing for a more sustainable, donor-centric approach.

Over the past decade, Lisa has hosted nearly 200 charitable salons at her Beverly Hills home, fostering real connections between nonprofits, donors, and the community. She currently serves on the California State Commission on the Status of Women and Girls, sits on the boards of ALLMEP (The Alliance for Middle East Peace), MatchNice, Jewish Story Partners, and New Jewish Narrative, and has held leadership roles with Cedars-Sinai Board of Governors, the New Israel Fund, and the Jewish Community Foundation of Los Angeles. Other board participation includes the L.A. District Attorney’s Crime Prevention Foundation, Make-a-Wish of Greater Los Angeles, and Girl Scouts of Greater Los Angeles, among others.

Lisa’s career spans leadership in media, business, and philanthropy, including time as a studio executive at NBC and Universal Studios. Now a doctoral candidate at the Lilly Family School of Philanthropy, Lisa is committed to ending the nonprofit starvation cycle and ensuring a more honest, impactful philanthropic culture.

A Los Angeles native, Lisa holds degrees from UCLA and Pepperdine and is the proud mother of three Millennials, two Gen Z’ers, and two dogs.

";}}

Are you overlooking donors who are ready to make a transformational impact?

From leveraging donor data to spotting key indicators of giving capacity and intent, this webinar will review industry data and proven strategies for uncovering major gift and legacy donors hiding in plain sight.

a:5:{s:6:"item-0";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:71:"Techniques for identifying high-capacity donors within your database ";}s:6:"item-4";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:67:"Key data points and behaviors that signal major giving potential ";}s:6:"item-3";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:73:"Strategies for targeting high-potential donors outside of your database ";}s:6:"item-2";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:63:"Using donation matching to engage, retain, and acquire donors ";}s:6:"item-1";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:8:"And more";}}
Whether you’re just starting your major gift program or looking to take it to the next level, this webinar will help you unlock new possibilities for growth and impact.

item.title: Unlocking Board Potential: Activating Volunteers for Fundraising Success
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn how to engage & activate board members as volunteers to drive fundraising success & strengthen your nonprofit.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Unlock Board Potential for Fundraising Success
Learn how to engage & activate board members as volunteers to drive fundraising success & strengthen your nonprofit.

a:2:{s:6:"item-0";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"9842";s:14:"presenter_name";s:15:"Chris Baiocchi ";s:21:"presenter_description";s:40:"Founder & CEO, Resolute Philanthropy";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:1342:"

Chris Baiocchi has worked in the nonprofit space for 25 years as a communicator, fundraiser, and leader. He’s led annual giving campaigns, planned special events, written grants, and helped fundraisers and nonprofit professionals find joy, innovation, and passion in their work.

Chris is the founder and CEO of Resolute Philanthropy – a consulting firm that provides nonprofit leaders with the tools and resources they need to thrive – because a kinder, safer, healthier world needs engaged and effective nonprofits.

Chris has worked alongside organizations such as Oasis Youth Shelter, Gig Harbor Waterfront Alliance, Habitat for Humanity of Orange County, and St. Joseph Hospital. He holds a master’s in organizational leadership from Brandman University and a bachelor’s in English/journalism from Chapman University.

Chris and his family live in Tacoma, Washington. He is an active reader, an occasional cook, and loves bow ties, icebreakers, and heated arguments about the best sci-fi franchise.

 

";}s:6:"item-1";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"9675";s:14:"presenter_name";s:16:"Madison Kautman ";s:21:"presenter_description";s:74:"Senior Marketing Campaign Manager, Givesmart powered by Momentive Software";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:1207:"

Madison Kautman joined the GiveSmart team over four years ago and is a fundraising professional with over eight years of experience working with nonprofits. Before her time with GiveSmart, Madison worked with numerous nonprofit organizations, including art galleries, art museums, children’s museums, and healthcare clinics. Through her nonprofit work, she spent about two years as a GiveSmart customer and has extensive experience utilizing the platform to facilitate events and fundraisers.

At her core, Madison is someone who loves to work for a mission that makes a measurable impact. In her current role, she is passionate about helping nonprofits and providing them with the resources they need to continue impacting our communities every day.

";}}

Your board members are more than advisors—they’re powerful partners in advancing your mission.

But when it comes to fundraising, many nonprofits struggle to activate their board members beyond governance and into active engagement. This webinar will explore how to inspire, equip, and activate your board members as confident fundraisers and mission stewards, especially during the critical year-end giving season.

Whether you’re building a culture of philanthropy or looking to energize your board for year-end, this session will give you actionable strategies to unlock your board’s full potential and raise more together. Watch now!

item.title: Psychology-Driven Learner Engagement Strategies
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover psychology-driven learner engagement strategies to boost participation, motivation, & completion using proven learning principles.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Understanding what drives learners to participate, persist, and succeed is at the heart of effective online education. This webinar explores the psychological principles behind learner motivation, offering practical tips to keep your audience engaged from the first module to the final assessment. Discover how gamification elements like badges and achievement milestones tap into intrinsic reward systems to boost completion rates and build lasting learning habits. You'll also explore how the power of social learning deepens understanding and creates a sense of belonging. Join us for a hands-on look at how credentialing, test engine, and discussion/community features work together to create a psychologically rich, results-driven learning environment.

item.title: The Essential Volunteer: Harness the Power of Your Volunteers to Ensure Resiliency
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover how to harness the power of volunteers in our on-demand webinar. Learn strategies to recruit, engage, and retain volunteers for lasting impact.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: The Essential Volunteer: Harness the Power of Your Volunteers to Ensure Resiliency
Discover how to harness the power of volunteers in our on-demand webinar. Learn strategies to recruit, engage, and retain volunteers for lasting impact.

Volunteers are more important than ever in sustaining small associations. But how do you recruit, engage, and retain them effectively? Join us for an insightful webinar where we’ll explore how YourMembership AMS can help you build a thriving volunteer program that enhances member engagement and strengthens your organization’s resiliency.

We’ll also showcase how YourMembership’s all-in-one AMS solution empowers you to create seamless volunteer experiences while saving your staff time and effort.  

Whether you’re looking to revamp your current volunteer program or build one from the ground up, this short webinar is packed with actionable insights to help your association succeed.  

Don’t miss this opportunity to unlock the full potential of your volunteers and drive lasting impact for your organization! Register today!
This webinar is intended for those looking for a new association management software solution.

item.title: Accelerating AI Thinking and Readiness at AMCs
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Join our webinar for practical steps to accelerate AI thinking & readiness at your AMC. Learn how to move from curiosity to adoption.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: AI Readiness for AMCs: Practical Steps to Get Started
Join our webinar for practical steps to accelerate AI thinking & readiness at your AMC. Learn how to move from curiosity to adoption.

a:1:{s:6:"item-0";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:5:"10598";s:14:"presenter_name";s:14:"Rich Vallaster";s:21:"presenter_description";s:15:"CEM, QAS, AAiP ";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:249:"

Senior Director of Industry Strategy at Momentive Software

";}}

Many Association Management Companies remain stuck between basic experimentation and simple implementation of Artificial Intelligence (AI). Now is the time to create a clear, strategic path to AI value for both your AMC and your clients. 

We’ll move beyond surface-level conversations about tools and explore how AMCs can accelerate AI adoption both internally and externally. This session, tailored for all experience levels, provides practical insights into AI readiness and AMC-specific strategies. Discover how AMCs can leverage AI to boost staff efficiency, uncover deeper insights, develop AI-driven products, and make smarter decisions that impress clients and set themselves apart from the competition. 

Attendees will gain actionable tips, see live demonstrations, and experience engaging moments that reveal how every role within an AMC can benefit from embracing AI and challenge you to rethink your approach.  

Live attendees will receive a bonus copy of our AMC AI Readiness Guide, a playbook for scaling and productizing AI across client portfolios.

item.title: How to Avoid Common AMS Buying Pitfalls & Red Flags
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Shopping for a new AMS? Learn how associations can avoid common tech buying pitfalls and choose the right platform with confidence.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: AMS Buying Pitfalls & Red Flags to Avoid
Avoid costly AMS mistakes. Learn the key red flags & pitfalls to watch for when evaluating & selecting an association management system.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:5:"10909";s:14:"presenter_name";s:11:"Bria Thomas";s:21:"presenter_description";s:104:"Associate Director, Operations and Special Projects, National Community Pharmacists Association (NCPA) ";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-1";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:5:"10903";s:14:"presenter_name";s:11:"Debra Lally";s:21:"presenter_description";s:88:"CAE, Executive Director, National Association of Emergency Medical Technicians (NAEMT) ";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}s:6:"item-2";a:4:{s:15:"presenter_photo";s:4:"2750";s:14:"presenter_name";s:15:"Chris Capistran";s:21:"presenter_description";s:57:"VP of Channel and Strategic Accounts, Momentive Software ";s:13:"presenter_bio";s:0:"";}}

If you plan to invest in a new association management solution (AMS) this year, ensure your organization is set up for long‑term success, not costly setbacks. 

Selecting a new AMS is complex and risky. From misaligned stakeholders to unclear requirements and overwhelming vendor options, many associations fall into common traps that delay progress and drain resources. 

This expert‑led webinar will show you how to avoid the most common association tech buying pitfalls and approach your AMS evaluation with clarity, confidence, a proven framework, and next steps you can apply immediately to your buying process.
In this session, you’ll learn how to:

a:4:{s:6:"item-0";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:64:"Know when it’s time to invest in new association technology ";}s:6:"item-1";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:76:"Avoid process, data, and adoption challenges and delays before they start ";}s:6:"item-2";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:71:"Align the right stakeholders and define clear goals and requirements ";}s:6:"item-3";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:67:"Evaluate vendors, demos, and proposals without getting overwhelmed ";}}
Whether you’re early in your research or actively evaluating vendors, this session will help you make a smarter, more strategic technology decision—and avoid mistakes that can derail your investment.
